README.title: MLBoss Documentation Index
README.description: This index is the single authoritative table of contents for all Markdown docs in the project. Each entry has a one-sentence description; avoid duplicating content across files.

dashboard-components.title: Dashboard Component System
dashboard-components.description: The dashboard is built from **self-contained React components** ("cards") rendered inside a flexible grid.

data-architecture.title: Data Architecture
data-architecture.description: This is the canonical reference for how MLBoss fetches, models, and composes data. Read this before touching anything in `src/lib/fantasy/`, `src/lib/mlb/`, `src/lib/roster/`, `src/lib/pitching/`, or `src/lib/lineup/`.

design-system.title: MLBoss Design System
design-system.description: This document defines the visual design standards for MLBoss, including colors, typography, and component patterns. All design tokens are implemented as CSS variables and wired into Tailwind CSS utilities.

for-ai-developers.title: For AI Developers
for-ai-developers.description: This guide is specifically written for LLMs working on the MLBoss codebase. It contains documentation strategy and key patterns to follow.

mlb-api-reference.title: MLB Stats API Reference
mlb-api-reference.description: The MLB Stats API (`https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1`) is the public, unauthenticated data source MLBoss uses for everything the Yahoo Fantasy API can't give us: probable pitchers, player stats, handedness splits, team offense profiles, venues, weather, and player identity.

product-spec.title: MLBoss: Fantasy Baseball Decision Support System
product-spec.description: MLBoss is a fantasy baseball decision support tool designed to help users make informed daily lineup and pitcher-streaming decisions through comprehensive data analysis. The system provides parallel batter and pitcher analysis surfaces backed by fantasy league-wide context for strategic decision-making.

scoring-conventions.title: Scoring Conventions
scoring-conventions.description: This is the reference for HOW the app turns raw stats into the talent ratings, matchup ratings, and streaming verdicts surfaced in the UI. Read this before adding a new category, tuning a constant, or writing a second function that "estimates how good a player is".

setup.title: Setup & Configuration
setup.description: This document is the single source of truth for all environment variables and the Yahoo OAuth setup required to run MLBoss.

stats.title: Statistical Data Architecture
stats.description: MLBoss uses `stat_id` as the canonical identifier for all player and team statistics. This numeric key system eliminates ambiguity between similar statistics (like pitcher vs batter strikeouts) and provides consistent identification across all API calls.

streaming-page.title: Streaming Page Architecture
streaming-page.description: The `/streaming` page is MLBoss's dedicated pitcher-pickup tool. It is **not** a daily lineup surface — daily pitcher sit/start lives on the Today page (`/lineup`, Pitchers tab). Streaming is about rotating through the ~6 moves-per-week budget on the 1-2 bench pitcher slots that rotate, with visibility multiple days ahead.

ui-patterns.title: UI Patterns & Shared Components
ui-patterns.description: Before building new UI, check this inventory. Reuse existing patterns and shared components rather than creating new ones.
